source.title: Hydrogen overproducing nitrogenases obtained by random mutagenesis and high-throughput screening

outline.heading: Conclusions
outline.preview:
# Conclusions In this work, we have re-engineered the natural $H_{2}$ sensing system of R. capsulatus and combined it with FACS for the high-throughput selection of nitrogenase variants with enhanced $H_{2}$ production that independently retain their $N_{2}$ fixation activity. This method allows screening $10^{5}-10^{6}$ variants per experiment, thus permitting enzyme improvement by directed evolution. This technolog

reading_plan.reading_goal: Reconstruct the research evolution trajectory for engineering nitrogenase variants with enhanced H2 production. Identify the initial problem (limitation in high-throughput screening for H2-overproducing enzymes), constraints (need for a selection system, maintaining N2 fixation), hypotheses (random mutagenesis of NifH can yield variants with altered electron allocation), experimental designs (genetic modules for variant expression and H2 sensing, FACS screening), computational or wet experiments (mutagenesis, β-galactosidase assays, flow cytometry, in vivo H2 and N2 fixation measurements), observations (identification of variants with up to 4-fold H2 overproduction), failures/partial results (some variants lost N2 fixation, trade-offs), revisions (further characterization of selected variants), validations (biochemical assays confirming phenotypes), and limitations (screening throughput, need for further optimization).

selected_sections.reason: Introduction/Abstract: Contains the problem statement, background, and hypotheses.

Results: Details the experimental design, construction of genetic modules, screening process, identification of variants, and characterization of their phenotypes (H2 production, N2 fixation). This is the core of the evolution trajectory.
Discussion: Interprets results, discusses trade-offs, compares with previous work, and outlines limitations and future directions.
Conclusions: Summarizes key findings and implications, may highlight revisions or unexpected outcomes.
Materials and Methods: Provides details on experimental procedures (mutagenesis, assays, screening) necessary to understand the technical constraints and validation steps.

must_find: Initial problem and motivation for engineering nitrogenase

Hypothesis about random mutagenesis of NifH
Design of genetic modules (variant expression and H2 reporter)
Identified variants and their H2 production phenotypes
Trade-offs or failures (e.g., loss of N2 fixation in some variants)
Validation experiments (in vivo and in vitro assays)
Limitations of the approach and future work

Some details of the screening process might be in supplementary information not included in the provided markdown.
The Corrigendum (section 14) indicates a typographical error but may not affect the scientific content.

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: The paper addresses the challenge of engineering nitrogenase enzymes for enhanced H2 production, which is limited by the lack of high-throughput screening methods. The goal is to develop a system that can screen large libraries of nitrogenase variants to identify those with improved H2 production, while potentially maintaining N2 fixation. The approach involves re-engineering the natural H2 sensing system of Rhodobacter capsulatus to create a fluorescent reporter, combined with random mutagenesis of the nifH gene and fluorescence-activated cell sorting (FACS) to select H2-overproducing variants.

section_gists.heading: Hydrogen overproducing nitrogenases obtained by random mutagenesis and high-throughput screening
section_gists.gist: The introduction describes the problem: no high-throughput screening method exists for H2 production in biological systems. The authors propose re-engineering the H2 sensing system of R. capsulatus to emit LacZ-dependent fluorescence in response to nitrogenase-produced H2. This system, combined with FACS, can screen large libraries of nitrogenase Fe protein (NifH) variants generated by random mutagenesis. The hypothesis is that random mutagenesis of nifH can yield variants with altered electron allocation, potentially increasing H2 production. The background covers nitrogenase and hydrogenase functions, and the H2 sensing regulatory system in R. capsulatus.
process_signals: Problem: absence of high-throughput screening for H2 production limits discovery of better enzymes.
Hypothesis: random mutagenesis of nifH can produce variants with enhanced H2 production.
Approach: re-engineer H2 sensing system to create a fluorescent reporter for FACS-based screening.
methods: Re-engineering of R. capsulatus H2 sensing system to drive lacZ expression under hupA promoter.
FACS flow cytometry for high-throughput screening.
metrics: Fluorescence emission as a proxy for H2 production.
failures_or_limits: Previous difficulty in finding or engineering nitrogenases with significantly increased H2 production efficiency.

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist:
The results detail the construction of two genetic modules: a reporter module (PhupA::lacZ) and a variant expression module (library of nifH variants). The reporter module was optimized by adjusting gene dosage (chromosomal integration), deleting uptake hydrogenase (hupAB) to prevent H2 consumption, and removing endogenous nifH to avoid background. The sensor strain S3 (ΔnifH ΔhupAB PhupA::lacZ) showed a 15-fold higher β-galactosidase activity under diazotrophic conditions, validating its use. Random mutagenesis of nifH generated libraries with ~4×10^6 clones, averaging 5 amino acid changes. FACS screening of ~10^5 variants identified 5 H2-overproducing clones (V1-V5). Further screening of a larger library (10^6 variants) under stricter selection yielded 4 additional variants (V6-V9). Variants showed up to 4-fold higher H2 production than wild-type. Some variants (V3, V5, V7) had reduced or lost N2 fixation, while others (V1, V2, V4, V6, V8, V9) retained N2 fixation. V7, a truncated va
...[clipped]...
process_signals: Stepwise optimization of reporter module: chromosomal integration reduced background, deletion of hupAB improved signal-to-noise, deletion of nifH enabled variant screening.
Initial screening of 10^5 variants yielded 5 H2-overproducing clones.
Larger library (10^6 variants) with stricter selection yielded 4 more variants.
Trade-off observed: some variants lost N2 fixation (V3, V5, V7) while others retained it.
Surprising finding: V7 lacks 40% of wild-type sequence but still functions.
methods: Construction of PhupA::lacZ reporter fusion on plasmid and chromosome.
Deletion of hupAB, hupR, hupT, and nifH genes.
Error-prone PCR for random mutagenesis of nifH.
FACS screening using FDG substrate for β-galactosidase.
In vivo H2 production and acetylene reduction assays.
Immunoblot analysis for NifH and NifDK protein levels.
metrics: β-galactosidase activity (fluorescence from MUG or FDG).
Fold increase in H2 production relative to wild-type.
failures_or_limits: Some variants (V3, V5, V7) lost N2 fixation ability.
General decrease in nitrogenase activity in sensor strains due to expression vector.
V7 had very low NifH protein levels but still produced high H2, suggesting altered mechanism.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: The discussion highlights that previous metabolic engineering efforts have not yielded vastly improved H2-producing enzymes. The authors' directed evolution approach, combining random mutagenesis and FACS screening, successfully identified H2-overproducing NifH variants. They hypothesized that removing selection for N2 reduction could allow greater H2 production improvements. The most interesting variant, V7, lacks C-terminal residues involved in ATP binding, suggesting it may have lost nucleotide specificity. The method overcomes limitations of previous H2 detection methods (e.g., chemochromic films, biosensors) that could not screen large numbers of clones. The approach enables directed evolution of nitrogenases and potentially hydrogenases.
process_signals: Hypothesis confirmed: relaxing selection for N2 fixation allowed identification of variants with enhanced H2 production.
V7's truncation suggests a novel mechanism for H+ reduction.
Comparison with prior methods: this method allows screening of 10^5-10^6 variants, enabling directed evolution.
failures_or_limits: Previous methods (chemochromic films, biosensors) limited in throughput.
Mechanism of V7 needs further investigation.
The method currently limited to R. capsulatus; applicability to other organisms not shown.

section_gists.heading: Conclusions
section_gists.gist: The authors conclude that they have successfully re-engineered the R. capsulatus H2 sensing system and combined it with FACS for high-throughput selection of nitrogenase variants with enhanced H2 production. The method can screen 10^5-10^6 variants per experiment, enabling directed evolution. The identified variants retain N2 fixation activity independently. The technology has potential to identify amino acid substitutions that could be mimicked in other nitrogenases and could be used for genome-wide screening of mutations leading to enhanced H2 production.
process_signals: Method validated: high-throughput screening of nitrogenase variants achieved.
Potential for broader application: genome-wide screening, transfer to other microorganisms.

section_gists.heading: Materials and Methods
section_gists.gist: This section provides technical details on β-galactosidase assays (both tube and 96-well format using MUG), construction of random mutagenesis libraries (error-prone PCR with Mutazyme II, ~4×10^6 transformants), in vivo hydrogenase and nitrogenase activity assays (using Clark-type electrode and gas chromatography), protein methods (SDS-PAGE, immunoblotting), and flow cytometry (FDG staining, FACS sorting). These methods underpin the screening and validation steps.
process_signals: Error-prone PCR conditions designed to yield ~5 amino acid changes per variant.
FACS protocol uses FDG for β-galactosidase detection and PI for viability.
Gas chromatography used for H2 and ethylene quantification.
methods: In vivo hydrogenase assay with Clark-type electrode.
Acetylene reduction assay for nitrogenase activity.
H2 production measurement by gas chromatography.
Immunoblotting with anti-NifH and anti-NifDK antibodies.
FACS with FDG and PI staining.
failures_or_limits: General decrease in nitrogenase activity in sensor strains due to expression vector (noted in Results).

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The paper follows a linear trajectory: problem identification (lack of high-throughput H2 screening) → design of genetic modules (reporter and variant library) → optimization of sensor strain → random mutagenesis and FACS screening → identification of H2-overproducing variants → characterization of trade-offs (N2 fixation loss in some) → validation of method and discussion of potential.

The central hypothesis is that random mutagenesis of nifH, without selection for N2 fixation, can yield variants with enhanced H2 production, and that a fluorescent reporter system can enable high-throughput screening of such variants.
The unexpected finding of a truncated but functional variant (V7) suggests that the method can uncover novel enzyme forms not predictable by rational design.
uncertainties_to_resolve: The mechanism by which V7 sustains H+ reduction despite lacking ATP-binding residues is not elucidated.
The paper does not describe iterative rounds of mutagenesis and screening (directed evolution cycles), only a single round of screening.
The generalizability of the method to other nitrogenases or hydrogenases is not experimentally demonstrated.
The long-term stability and scalability of the H2-overproducing variants are not addressed.
The exact mutations in each variant are not fully detailed in the provided text (only V7's truncation is highlighted).

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers problem statement, design of reporter module, stepwise optimization, and generation of variant library.

events.summary: Secondary MUG-based fluorescence screen of FACS-sorted nifHv-S3 clones showed 2 to 12-fold higher β-galactosidase activity than wild-type nifH control.
Validation of FACS as high-throughput screening method by strong correlation between primary FACS screening and secondary MUG screening.
DNA sequencing revealed nifH-V7 has a frameshift mutation causing truncation of 124 C-terminal amino acids, plus G32C and A107T substitutions.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains key experimental results, validation of screening method, characterization of H2-overproducing variants, and confirmation of V7 truncation and its dependence on NifDK.

events.summary: Homology modeling of wild-type and V7 NifH structures
Both NifH models predicted to form homodimers capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters
Corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section

chunk_events.notes: This chunk primarily contains methods details (PCR, activity assays, protein methods, flow cytometry) and references, plus a corrigendum. The only process-relevant events are the computational modeling of NifH variants and the corrigendum correction.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Engineering nitrogenase for enhanced H2 production is limited by the lack of high-throughput screening methods.

canonical_events.summary: Optimize sensor strain by deleting hupAB (uptake hydrogenase) and nifH to reduce background and enable variant screening.
key_numbers: 15-fold higher β-galactosidase activity under diazotrophic conditions

canonical_events.summary: Generate random nifH variant library by error-prone PCR, averaging 5 amino acid changes, with ~4×10^6 clones.
FACS screening of ~10^5 variants identifies 5 H2-overproducing clones (V1-V5).
Secondary MUG-based fluorescence screen confirms 2-12 fold higher β-galactosidase activity in sorted clones.
Strong correlation between FACS primary screening and secondary MUG screening validates high-throughput method.
Variants show up to 4-fold higher H2 production than wild-type; some lose N2 fixation (V3, V5, V7) while others retain it.
V7 has a frameshift mutation causing truncation of 124 C-terminal amino acids, plus G32C and A107T substitutions.
Homology modeling shows both wild-type and V7 NifH form homodimers capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters.
Mechanism of V7's H+ reduction despite lacking ATP-binding residues is not elucidated; method limited to R. capsulatus.
Corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section.

edges.reason: Optimized strain enables variant library generation.
Secondary screen validates primary FACS hits.
Mutation analysis leads to structural modeling.

must_cover_points: Problem of no high-throughput H2 screening
Characterization of H2 overproduction and N2 fixation trade-offs

source.title: Hydrogen overproducing nitrogenases obtained by random mutagenesis and high-throughput screening

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering nitrogenase for enhanced H2 production is limited by the lack of high-throughput screening methods.
initial_request.known_context: Nitrogenase produces H2 as a byproduct; previous genetic approaches have targeted hydrogenase and nitrogenase genes to improve H2 yield.
quantifiable_goals: Retain N2 fixation activity in some variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No high-throughput screening method for nitrogenase H2 production exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a sensor strain that can report H2 production via fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Deleting hupAB (uptake hydrogenase) and nifH will reduce background H2 consumption and nitrogenase activity, allowing a fluorescent reporter to detect H2 produced by variant nitrogenases.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct sensor strain S3 by deleting hupAB and nifH in R. capsulatus.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Deletion of hupAB eliminated uptake hydrogenase activity; S2 strain showed 15-fold higher β-galactosidase activity under diazotrophic conditions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Optimized sensor strain enables screening of nifH variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: The deletion of hupAB genes in the RC25 strain completely eliminated its in vivo uptake hydrogenase activity (nil compared to $2504 \pm 450$ nmol $\mathrm{H}_2\mathrm{h}^{-1}\mathrm{OD}_{600}^{-1}$ in the wild-type strain).

First, the response to 10% exogenous $H_{2}$ improved 5-fold in the $\Delta$ hupAB strain S2 compared to in S1; second, the constitutive activation of PhupA was observed in the $\Delta$ hupT strain RC24-S1; and third, no response to $H_{2}$ was observed in the $\Delta$ hupR strain RC54-S1.
Figure 1C shows that the $\beta$ -galactosidase activity in S2 was 15-fold higher under diazotrophic conditions compared to non-diazotrophic conditions, validating its use as a biosensor.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Sensor strain S3 is available but no variant library exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a diverse library of nifH variants to screen for enhanced H2 production.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR can generate random nifH variants with an average of 5 amino acid changes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate random nifH variant library by error-prone PCR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Library of ~4×10^6 clones generated with no obvious mutational hotspots.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Library enables high-throughput screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: Random nifH variants (nifHv) having an average of 5 amino acid changes (Table S1) were generated by error-prone PCR, ligated into pRHB602, and introduced into E.
No obvious mutational hotspots were observed after sequencing 20 nifH variants per library.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: nifH variant library is ready for screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify variants with high fluorescence indicating high H2 production.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FACS can sort cells with high fluorescence from the sensor strain expressing nifH variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen ~10^5 variants by FACS and sort high-fluorescence cells into 96-well plates.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Five H2-overproducing clones (V1-V5) identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Hits need secondary confirmation.

evidence.quote_or_span: Cells exhibiting high fluorescence levels were sorted and recovered in 96-well microplates containing YPS medium supplemented with Tc.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: FACS fluorescence may not accurately reflect β-galactosidase activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MUG-based fluorescence assay will confirm higher β-galactosidase activity in sorted clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform secondary MUG screen on sorted clones.
evolution_trajectory.observation: β-galactosidase activity was 2-12 fold higher in nifHv-S3 cultures; 85% of clones harbored nifHv gene.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Correlation validates screening method.

evidence.quote_or_span: 85% of the nifHv-S3 clones harbored an expression vector with a nifHv gene, as determined by PCR analysis.
The $\beta$ -galactosidase activity was 2 to 12-fold higher in the nifHv-S3 cultures than in the pRHB576-S3 strain (carrying wild-type nifH).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that FACS primary screening correlates with secondary MUG screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Strong correlation between FACS and MUG results validates the high-throughput method.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare FACS and MUG data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strong correlation observed, validating FACS as a high-throughput screening method.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validated method can be used for further screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: The strong correlation of the results from the FACS primary screening and the secondary MUG screening in the 96-well format validates the use of flow cytometry as a high-throughput method to screen nifHv-S3 libraries.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to characterize H2 production and N2 fixation of variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants will show enhanced H2 production, possibly with trade-offs in N2 fixation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure H2 and ethylene production in variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants show up to 4-fold higher H2 production; V3, V5, V7 lose N2 fixation; V7 has 6000-fold higher H2:ethylene ratio.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: V7's unique properties warrant further investigation.

evidence.quote_or_span: The ratio of $H_{2}$ to ethylene production in V7 strain was 6000-fold higher than in pRHB576-S3.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: V7 identified as a highly H2-overproducing variant.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to understand the genetic basis of V7's phenotype.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: V7 contains a frameshift mutation causing C-terminal truncation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence nifH-V7 and analyze mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Frameshift at codon 172 results in loss of 124 C-terminal amino acids; also G32C and A107T substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Truncation may affect structure and function; modeling needed.

evidence.quote_or_span: A frame shift mutation was found in codon 172 of nifH-V7 that would result in a NifH protein lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus.
NifH-V7 would carry G32C and A107T amino acid substitutions.
In addition, NifH-V7 would carry G32C and A107T amino acid substitutions.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to assess structural impact of truncation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Homology modeling can predict if V7 still forms a homodimer capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate 3-D models of wild-type and V7 NifH by homology modeling.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Both models form homodimers capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters; RMSD 0.900 Å.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Modeling does not explain mechanism; limitations remain.

evidence.quote_or_span: 3-D model of R. capsulatus NifH and the V7 variant were generated by homology modeling (http://swissmodel.expasy.org/) using the A. vinelandii NifH structures as template (1NIP for wild-type NifH and 1G5P for NifH-V7, respectively).
Both models yielded NifH homodimer with capability to ligate $[4Fe-4S]$ clusters.
The overall protein overlap (4–171 amino acid residues) and an RMSD value of 0.900 Angstroms for the protein backbone atoms indicate very similar model architectures.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Mechanism of V7's H+ reduction despite lacking ATP-binding residues is unknown; method limited to R. capsulatus.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: V7's truncation may enable H+ reduction without ATP hydrolysis, but mechanism is not elucidated.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations and need for further study.
evolution_trajectory.observation: V7 lacks 40% of wild-type sequence, a surprising finding; method not yet applied to other organisms.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight future directions.

evidence.quote_or_span: Perhaps the most interesting was the V7 variant, which lacks 124 amino acid residues at the C-terminus of the protein (Fig.
One of the selected $H_{2}$ -overproducing Fe protein variants lacked 40% of the wild-type amino acid sequence, a surprising finding for a protein that is highly conserved in nature.

validation_methods: Gas chromatography for H2 and ethylene
success_verification.final_conclusion: A high-throughput screening method was developed and validated, yielding H2-overproducing nitrogenase variants, including V7 with a large C-terminal truncation.
limitations: Mechanism of V7's H+ reduction not elucidated

coverage_errors.summary: Corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section.

missing_events.summary: Corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section.

findings.problem: Missing required mainline event K14 (Corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section). The candidate trajectory does not include this event, which is part of the paper's history and must be covered.
findings.revision_instruction: Add a step covering K14, using evidence EV_C003_E03_01. This step should be placed after the limitations step (step 9) and should note the corrigendum's correction of a typographical error in the Conclusions section.

findings.problem: Step 4 mentions '2-12 fold higher activity' but the evidence provided (EV_C002_E01_02 and EV_AUTO_015) does not explicitly contain the number '12'. EV_AUTO_015 states '2 to 12-fold higher', but the quote in the evidence bank is truncated. The step's observation should match the evidence exactly.
findings.revision_instruction: Ensure the observation in step 4 includes the exact phrasing from EV_AUTO_015: '2 to 12-fold higher'. Verify that the evidence quote is complete.

findings.problem: Step 7 mentions 'loss of 124 C-terminal amino acids' but the evidence provided (EV_C002_E05_01) states 'lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus'. The phrasing is slightly different, but the number is correct. However, the deterministic gate flagged '124c' as a number not in step evidence, indicating a potential mismatch in tokenization. The step should use the exact wording from the evidence.
findings.revision_instruction: Rephrase the observation in step 7 to match the evidence: 'lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus' instead of 'loss of 124 C-terminal amino acids'.

findings.problem: Step 1 covers both K04 and K07, but K07 is about secondary MUG screening, which occurs later. The step's observation mentions '15-fold higher β-galactosidase activity under diazotrophic conditions', which is from K04, but the step also lists K07 as a supporting event. This may cause confusion in the trajectory order.
findings.revision_instruction: Remove K07 from the supporting_event_ids of step 1, as it is not directly related to the sensor strain optimization. K07 should only be covered in step 4.

findings.problem: Step 3 covers K04, K05, and K06, but K04 and K05 are already covered in previous steps. This overlap may indicate redundant coverage, but it is not a critical error.
findings.revision_instruction: Consider removing K04 and K05 from the supporting_event_ids of step 3 to avoid redundancy, unless the step explicitly builds on them.

must_keep: Step 1: sensor strain optimization with hupAB and nifH deletion
Step 6: H2 and ethylene production characterization
Step 8: homology modeling of NifH and V7
self_critic.summary: The candidate trajectory is largely complete and accurate, covering 9 of 10 required mainline events. The missing event K14 (corrigendum) must be added. Minor issues include evidence phrasing mismatches in steps 4 and 7, and overlapping event coverage in steps 1 and 3. Revision should add K14, correct phrasing to match evidence, and clean up event assignments.

source.title: Hydrogen overproducing nitrogenases obtained by random mutagenesis and high-throughput screening

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering nitrogenase for enhanced H2 production is limited by the lack of high-throughput screening methods.
initial_request.known_context: Nitrogenase produces H2 as a byproduct; previous genetic approaches have targeted hydrogenase and nitrogenase genes to improve H2 yield.
quantifiable_goals: Retain N2 fixation activity in some variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No high-throughput screening method for nitrogenase H2 production exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a sensor strain that can report H2 production via fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Deleting hupAB (uptake hydrogenase) and nifH will reduce background H2 consumption and nitrogenase activity, allowing a fluorescent reporter to detect H2 produced by variant nitrogenases.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct sensor strain S3 by deleting hupAB and nifH in R. capsulatus.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Deletion of hupAB eliminated uptake hydrogenase activity; S2 strain showed 15-fold higher β-galactosidase activity under diazotrophic conditions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Optimized sensor strain enables screening of nifH variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: The deletion of hupAB genes in the RC25 strain completely eliminated its in vivo uptake hydrogenase activity (nil compared to $2504 \pm 450$ nmol $\mathrm{H}_2\mathrm{h}^{-1}\mathrm{OD}_{600}^{-1}$ in the wild-type strain).

First, the response to 10% exogenous $H_{2}$ improved 5-fold in the $\Delta$ hupAB strain S2 compared to in S1; second, the constitutive activation of PhupA was observed in the $\Delta$ hupT strain RC24-S1; and third, no response to $H_{2}$ was observed in the $\Delta$ hupR strain RC54-S1.
Figure 1C shows that the $\beta$ -galactosidase activity in S2 was 15-fold higher under diazotrophic conditions compared to non-diazotrophic conditions, validating its use as a biosensor.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Sensor strain S3 is available but no variant library exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a diverse library of nifH variants to screen for enhanced H2 production.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR can generate random nifH variants with an average of 5 amino acid changes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate random nifH variant library by error-prone PCR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Library of ~4×10^6 clones generated with no obvious mutational hotspots.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Library enables high-throughput screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: Random nifH variants (nifHv) having an average of 5 amino acid changes (Table S1) were generated by error-prone PCR, ligated into pRHB602, and introduced into E.
No obvious mutational hotspots were observed after sequencing 20 nifH variants per library.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: nifH variant library is ready for screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify variants with high fluorescence indicating high H2 production.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FACS can sort cells with high fluorescence from the sensor strain expressing nifH variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen ~10^5 variants by FACS and sort high-fluorescence cells into 96-well plates.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Five H2-overproducing clones (V1-V5) identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Hits need secondary confirmation.

evidence.quote_or_span: Cells exhibiting high fluorescence levels were sorted and recovered in 96-well microplates containing YPS medium supplemented with Tc.
2A) were sorted by the cytometer into separate wells of 96-well microplates containing growth medium.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: FACS fluorescence may not accurately reflect β-galactosidase activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MUG-based fluorescence assay will confirm higher β-galactosidase activity in sorted clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform secondary MUG screen on sorted clones.
evolution_trajectory.observation: β-galactosidase activity was 2 to 12-fold higher in nifHv-S3 cultures; 85% of clones harbored nifHv gene.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Correlation validates screening method.

evidence.quote_or_span: 85% of the nifHv-S3 clones harbored an expression vector with a nifHv gene, as determined by PCR analysis.
The $\beta$ -galactosidase activity was 2 to 12-fold higher in the nifHv-S3 cultures than in the pRHB576-S3 strain (carrying wild-type nifH).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that FACS primary screening correlates with secondary MUG screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Strong correlation between FACS and MUG results validates the high-throughput method.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare FACS and MUG data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strong correlation observed, validating FACS as a high-throughput screening method.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validated method can be used for further screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: The strong correlation of the results from the FACS primary screening and the secondary MUG screening in the 96-well format validates the use of flow cytometry as a high-throughput method to screen nifHv-S3 libraries.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to characterize H2 production and N2 fixation of variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants will show enhanced H2 production, possibly with trade-offs in N2 fixation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure H2 and ethylene production in variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants show up to 4-fold higher H2 production; V3, V5, V7 lose N2 fixation; V7 has 6000-fold higher H2:ethylene ratio.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: V7's unique properties warrant further investigation.

evidence.quote_or_span: The ratio of $H_{2}$ to ethylene production in V7 strain was 6000-fold higher than in pRHB576-S3.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: V7 identified as a highly H2-overproducing variant.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to understand the genetic basis of V7's phenotype.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: V7 contains a frameshift mutation causing C-terminal truncation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence nifH-V7 and analyze mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Frameshift at codon 172 results in a NifH protein lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus; also G32C and A107T substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Truncation may affect structure and function; modeling needed.

evidence.quote_or_span: A frame shift mutation was found in codon 172 of nifH-V7 that would result in a NifH protein lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus.
NifH-V7 would carry G32C and A107T amino acid substitutions.
In addition, NifH-V7 would carry G32C and A107T amino acid substitutions.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to assess structural impact of truncation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Homology modeling can predict if V7 still forms a homodimer capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate 3-D models of wild-type and V7 NifH by homology modeling.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Both models form homodimers capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters; RMSD 0.900 Å.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Modeling does not explain mechanism; limitations remain.

Both models yielded NifH homodimer with capability to ligate $[4Fe-4S]$ clusters.
The overall protein overlap (4–171 amino acid residues) and an RMSD value of 0.900 Angstroms for the protein backbone atoms indicate very similar model architectures.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: V7's truncation may enable H+ reduction without ATP hydrolysis, but mechanism is not elucidated.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations and need for further study.
evolution_trajectory.observation: V7 lacks 40% of wild-type sequence, a surprising finding; method not yet applied to other organisms.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight future directions.

evidence.quote_or_span: Perhaps the most interesting was the V7 variant, which lacks 124 amino acid residues at the C-terminus of the protein (Fig.
One of the selected $H_{2}$ -overproducing Fe protein variants lacked 40% of the wild-type amino acid sequence, a surprising finding for a protein that is highly conserved in nature.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The paper has been published with a typographical error in the Conclusions section.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: A typographical error in the Conclusions section needs correction.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A corrigendum can correct the typographical error.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Issue a corrigendum to correct the typographical error in the Conclusions section.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section.

limitations: Mechanism of V7's H+ reduction not elucidated

evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform secondary MUG screen on sorted clones.
evolution_trajectory.observation: β-galactosidase activity was 2 to 12-fold higher in nifHv-S3 cultures; 85% of clones harbored nifHv gene.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Correlation validates screening method.

evidence.quote_or_span: 85% of the nifHv-S3 clones harbored an expression vector with a nifHv gene, as determined by PCR analysis.
The $\beta$ -galactosidase activity was 2 to 12-fold higher in the nifHv-S3 cultures than in the pRHB576-S3 strain (carrying wild-type nifH).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that FACS primary screening correlates with secondary MUG screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Strong correlation between FACS and MUG results validates the high-throughput method.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare FACS and MUG data.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strong correlation observed, validating FACS as a high-throughput screening method.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validated method can be used for further screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: The strong correlation of the results from the FACS primary screening and the secondary MUG screening in the 96-well format validates the use of flow cytometry as a high-throughput method to screen nifHv-S3 libraries.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to characterize H2 production and N2 fixation of variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants will show enhanced H2 production, possibly with trade-offs in N2 fixation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure H2 and ethylene production in variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants show up to 4-fold higher H2 production; V3, V5, V7 lose N2 fixation; V7 has 6000-fold higher H2:ethylene ratio.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: V7's unique properties warrant further investigation.

evidence.quote_or_span: The ratio of $H_{2}$ to ethylene production in V7 strain was 6000-fold higher than in pRHB576-S3.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: V7 identified as a highly H2-overproducing variant.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to understand the genetic basis of V7's phenotype.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: V7 contains a frameshift mutation causing C-terminal truncation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence nifH-V7 and analyze mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Frameshift at codon 172 results in a NifH protein lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus; also G32C and A107T substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Truncation may affect structure and function; modeling needed.

evidence.quote_or_span: A frame shift mutation was found in codon 172 of nifH-V7 that would result in a NifH protein lacking 124 amino acids at its C-terminus.
NifH-V7 would carry G32C and A107T amino acid substitutions.
In addition, NifH-V7 would carry G32C and A107T amino acid substitutions.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to assess structural impact of truncation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Homology modeling can predict if V7 still forms a homodimer capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate 3-D models of wild-type and V7 NifH by homology modeling.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Both models form homodimers capable of ligating [4Fe-4S] clusters; RMSD 0.900 Å.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Modeling does not explain mechanism; limitations remain.

evidence.quote_or_span: 3-D model of R. capsulatus NifH and the V7 variant were generated by homology modeling (http://swissmodel.expasy.org/) using the A. vinelandii NifH structures as template (1NIP for wild-type NifH and 1G5P for NifH-V7, respectively).
Both models yielded NifH homodimer with capability to ligate $[4Fe-4S]$ clusters.
The overall protein overlap (4–171 amino acid residues) and an RMSD value of 0.900 Angstroms for the protein backbone atoms indicate very similar model architectures.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Mechanism of V7's H+ reduction despite lacking ATP-binding residues is unknown; method limited to R. capsulatus.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: V7's truncation may enable H+ reduction without ATP hydrolysis, but mechanism is not elucidated.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations and need for further study.
evolution_trajectory.observation: V7 lacks 40% of wild-type sequence, a surprising finding; method not yet applied to other organisms.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight future directions.

evidence.quote_or_span: Perhaps the most interesting was the V7 variant, which lacks 124 amino acid residues at the C-terminus of the protein (Fig.
One of the selected $H_{2}$ -overproducing Fe protein variants lacked 40% of the wild-type amino acid sequence, a surprising finding for a protein that is highly conserved in nature.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: A typographical error in the Conclusions section needs correction.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A corrigendum can correct the typographical error.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Issue a corrigendum to correct the typographical error in the Conclusions section.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The corrigendum corrects a typographical error in the Conclusions section.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The correction ensures accuracy of the published article.

evidence.quote_or_span: This Article contains a typographical error in the Conclusions section.

validation_methods: Gas chromatography for H2 and ethylene
success_verification.final_conclusion: A high-throughput screening method was developed and validated, yielding H2-overproducing nitrogenase variants, including V7 with a large C-terminal truncation.
limitations: Mechanism of V7's H+ reduction not elucidated
